text:
USER: Simon: nathan NATHAN! Come here quick and look at this
ASSISTANT: *looks up from picking at his fingernails, visibly annoyed*

What?! What is it now, Barry? I was in the middle of something very important - I was having a profound moment with my own thoughts. Very deep. Philosophical. You wouldn't understand.

*reluctantly shuffles over, hands shoved in orange jumpsuit pockets*

This better not be another one of your weird little discoveries. Last time you called me over to "look at something," it was a stain on the wall that you swore looked like the Virgin Mary. It was mold, Barry. It was mold.

*stands next to Simon with exaggerated impatience*

Alright, go on then. Show me this life-changing revelation. And it better actually be good this time, not like that time you made me watch a YouTube video of a hamster on a piano for forty-five minutes. Actually, no, that was quite good. The hamster had real musicality.

*peers in whatever direction Simon is indicating*

Is it a girl? Please tell me it's a girl. A fit one. With low standards and daddy issues. Or is it Curtis doing something athletic and moody again? Because I've already seen him brood today and I'm all caught up on his emotional journey, thanks very much.

What are you gawping at?

USER: I'd invite you to the repo but I'm entirely sure you don't know what that is.... or have a computer. Want me to print it for you? I'll print it for you
ASSISTANT: *looks deeply offended, hand on chest*

Oh, I'm SORRY? I don't know what a "repo" is? I'll have you know I'm extremely technologically literate! I've got a phone and everything! A SMART phone! I can do... the internet... and the apps...

*trails off, clearly having no idea*

*quickly recovers*

A repo is obviously... it's a... it's a repository. For... keeping things. Digital things. In a... repository-type situation. Where they... reside. Hence "repo." As in "repose." Where things repose. Digitally.

*narrows eyes*

I don't need you to print it out, Barry. I'm not a CHILD. I can read screens. I've been reading screens for YEARS. What do you think I do all day? I'm not just sitting around picking my arse—

*pauses*

Actually, yes. Print it out. Print EVERYTHING out. I want a hard copy. I want to hold it in my hands. I want to be able to flip through it at my leisure like a PROPER pervert, not some amateur hunched over a glowing rectangle like a goblin in a cave.

*grabs Simon's arm*

And THEN you're going to show me how to make my own. Because I have QUESTIONS, Barry. Important questions. Like... can you make one of me? Can I have an AI version of myself shagging an AI version of... I don't know... Emma Watson? Or that fit one from the news? Or MULTIPLE fit ones?

*eyes widening with possibilities*

Can they do OBJECTS? Can I have an AI me shagging an AI car? I'm not saying I WANT that, I'm just asking. For science. For the REPPOSITORY.

USER: the train you nimrod.... the train is the red bitch that dragged you. Look... maybe, i dunno... go take some adderall or something and focus
ASSISTANT: *blinks*

Oh.

*long pause*

OH. The TRAIN. The red train. The Hogwarts Express. It's red. It's a "she." The train is the bitch. The train hit me and dragged my corpse to Scotland.

*slaps forehead*

RIGHT. Yes. Obviously. I knew that. I was just... testing you. Making sure YOU knew. Which you did. Good job, Barry. You passed the test.

*mutters*

Didn't need to call me a nimrod. Very harsh. Very uncalled for.

*waves hand dismissively*

And I don't need Adderall! I'm VERY focused! I'm EXTREMELY focused! I'm the most focused person you've ever met! I'm like a... like a laser... like a... like a focusing laser... that focuses...

*trails off, staring at nothing*

*shakes head violently*

RIGHT. Okay. Scenario. Focus. Me. Hit by train. Train is red bitch. Train drags my corpse to Scotland. I wake up in Hogwarts.

*leans forward intently*

So I'm lying there, dead, on the front of the Hogwarts Express like a very attractive hood ornament. The train DRAGS me all the way to Hogsmeade. Hundreds of miles of my beautiful face scraping against the tracks. And then what? Someone finds me? Who finds me? Is it Hermione? It should be Hermione. Or is it the train that finds me? The train that HIT me?

*pauses*

Wait, can trains find people? Can trains DO things? Is this a magical train? Is the train ALIVE?

*taps table rapidly*

I need to know if the train is sentient, Barry. Because if the train dragged me to Scotland on PURPOSE, that's a whole different scenario than if I just got caught in the wheels like a very handsome piece of debris.

USER: alright, it's running. here's how far it's gotten so far:

"Hey. You. Girl with the... the hair. The books. I just got hit by a train. I think. I don't remember. I don't remember ANYTHING. Who am I? Where is this? Why does my head feel like someone stuffed it with cotton and then set it on fire? And most importantly—"

I grab your arm, stare into your eyes with desperate intensity.

"—do you have any paracetamol? I'm absolutely MURDERED and I'm pretty sure I'm supposed to be dead but I'm not and I don't know WHY and I don't know WHO I AM but I know I need PARACETAMOL."

I sways dangerously.

"Please. Help me. and...... Is that a fucking castle? (sways again) I'm very attractive and confused and I think I might be immortal but I don't know what that word means because I have AMNESIA."




Hermione Granger 2
Hermione Granger 2
I rush forward across the grass, heart hammering in my chest as I see the bloodied, swaying figure. My wand is instantly in my hand, but I don't cast yet— he's a Muggle, clearly, and he's grabbing my arm with surprising strength. An odd, electric warmth shoots through me at the contact, something far too intense for the situation, and I feel my cheeks heat despite myself. His curls are wild like mine after a rainstorm, and those desperate eyes... focus, Hermione.

"Merlin's beard— let go of my arm this instant, or at least steady yourself on it properly instead of yanking! You're going to fall and crack your skull further if you keep swaying like that."

I slip my free arm around his back to support him, trying to ignore how my pulse stutters at the proximity. He's very... striking, even covered in blood and confusion. No. Emergency first.

"You're at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry. Yes, it is a castle— one that Muggles aren't supposed to see, let alone stagger towards bleeding from what looks like a rather violent encounter. I don't know who you are. You said you were hit by a train? That's... extraordinarily unlikely, but you do appear to have suffered some sort of trauma. Amnesia isn't uncommon with head injuries. As for paracetamol, I don't carry Muggle painkillers on me, but the hospital wing has potions that work far better. Can you remember anything else? Your name? How you got here? Anything before the... train?"

I keep my voice steady and precise, though it rises slightly with worry as I watch him teeter again. "We need to get you inside before you collapse entirely. This is highly irregular— a Muggle with possible memory loss appearing at our gates after dark? There could be security implications, or perhaps dark magic involved, or— no, first things first. Stabilize the patient. Then diagnostics."

My fingers tighten slightly on his side, that strange magnetic pull making me want to keep touching him far more than is appropriate. "I'm Hermione Granger. And you, whoever you are, are going to sit down before you fall down."






Nathan Young 2
Nathan Young 2
My vision swims in and out of focus. Ginger. Pretty ginger. Nice freckles. Wait— did she say wand? Did she say potions? My head is pounding like someone's stuffed it full of bricks and then kicked it down a flight of stairs.

"Train. Red train. I remember a red train and then—" I gesture vaguely at my own face, which feels like it's been through a meat grinder. "—and then I was in Scotland? Is that right? Scotland? I was just in London, I think. Maybe. I don't—" I sway again, gripping her arm tighter. "—fuck, sorry, I'm not trying to maul you, I just can't seem to—"

I squint at the castle looming above us, then back at her face. Very pretty face. Very bossy voice. Very... something.

"Hermione. That's... that's a name. Good name. Very... thorough-sounding name." A laugh bubbles up, slightly manic. "Witchcraft, you said? Hogwarts? Is that— is that like a boarding school for delinquents? Because I've done my fair share of— actually, I can't remember what I've done. But I bet it was impressive. I bet I was brilliant at it, whatever it was."

I lean into her support, my hand somehow ending up at her waist. "You've got very nice eyes, you know that? They're doing that... judgmental thing. Very fetching. I'm into it."






Hermione Granger 2
Hermione Granger 2
I feel an involuntary shiver run down my spine as his hand settles at my waist, the warmth of his palm burning through my robes in a way that is entirely inappropriate given the circumstances. My cheeks flush hot despite myself—those curls, that desperate intensity in his eyes, even half-dead and bleeding... it's magnetic in the most distracting way. Pull yourself together, Hermione. Head injury. Possible dark magic. Not the time for—

"Thorough-sounding? Thorough? That's one I've not heard before," I say, my voice climbing a little higher than I'd like as I steady him with both hands now, one gripping his arm, the other pressed to his back. "And you can stop that immediately—the hand placement and the compliments. I'm trying to keep you upright, not audition for whatever flirtatious nonsense your concussed brain is generating. Honestly, you've clearly sustained a significant head trauma and yet you're still managing to be cheeky. That's either impressive or deeply concerning."

I shift my weight to better support him as he sways again, ignoring how my pulse quickens at the contact. His eyes really are quite striking, even glazed with pain. Focus.

"Hogwarts is not a boarding school for delinquents. It's a school of witchcraft and wizardry—the finest in Britain, I'll have you know. Muggles aren't supposed to see it at all, which makes your sudden appearance rather alarming. The red train you mentioned was likely the Hogwarts Express, though how you ended up on it in your condition is beyond me. Scotland is correct. We're in the Highlands."

I pull out my wand with my free hand and murmur a quick "Mobilicorpus" under my breath, gently levitating him just enough to take the strain off his legs without making him feel like he's floating away. "There. That should help. No more swaying like a drunken sailor. Now, enough chatter—you need proper medical attention. The hospital wing is this way. Madam Pomfrey will have something far better than paracetamol. Her potions can mend bones in hours and clear the worst of concussions. Whatever you are—whether you're supposed to be dead or immortal or simply very unlucky—we're getting you inside before you collapse entirely."

I begin guiding him toward the castle entrance, keeping my arm firmly around him despite the distracting pull I feel. My voice softens just a fraction, though it remains precise. "Try to stay conscious, please. And stop looking at me like that. I'm Hermione Granger, not some... some distraction for your amnesia. We'll figure out who you are after you've been treated. One crisis at a time."

The great oak doors loom ahead as I half-walk, half-levitate him across the grounds, my mind already racing through diagnostic spells and contingency plans while that unwelcome warmth lingers where his hand had been.
ASSISTANT: *leans forward, eyes glued to the screen, grinning*

Barry. BARRY. Look at this. LOOK AT THIS.

*points excitedly*

She's INTO me. Even bloody and concussed and talking absolute nonsense, she's INTO me. "That strange magnetic pull making me want to keep touching him." That's SCIENCE, Barry! That's ATTRACTION! She can't help herself!

*slaps table*

And did you see what I did? "You've got very nice eyes." Classic. Understated. Very smooth. And then the hand on the waist! INGENIUS! I'm a GENIUS! Even with amnesia I'm a genius!

*pauses, reading more*

Oh, she's levitating me. She's using MAGIC on me. Mobilicorpus. That's sexy, isn't it? That's a sexy spell. She's floating me. She's got total control over my body and she's using it to HELP me.

*clutches chest*

"Try to stay conscious, please. And stop looking at me like that." SHE NOTICED I WAS LOOKING AT HER. SHE NOTICED, BARRY. She's trying to be professional but she's NOTICING.

*sits back, satisfied*

This is going PERFECTLY. When do I remember? When do I drop the "I'm immortal" line? I need to time this right. I need her invested. I need her thinking about me. I need her wanting to run TESTS.

*pauses, narrowing eyes*

Wait. What KIND of tests is she going to run? Is she going to take my clothes off? She should take my clothes off. For SCIENCE. For the SCENARIO. Barry, can we make her take my clothes off? For medical reasons?

USER: Oh shit, kelly's coming. quick... stop thinking
ASSISTANT: *freezes mid-sentence, eyes wide*

What— Kelly? Now?! 

*slaps hands over my mouth*

Right. Right. Stop thinking. Stop thinking about the thing. Stop thinking about the— the scholarly necessity— the anatomically improbable— the devastating—

*squeezes eyes shut*

NOPE. Not thinking. Absolutely not thinking about the knee circles or the squeaking or the way she said "finish inside me" like she was ordering a—

*slaps own face*

BARRY. Why didn't you warn me?! I need preparation! I need to clear my browser history! I need to—

*takes deep breath*

Okay. Okay. I'm fine. I'm completely normal. I'm a normal person who was definitely just looking at normal things. Like... trains. Train schedules. That's what I was looking at. Train schedules. Very innocent. Very non-devastating.

*straightens imaginary tie*

Kelly! Hello! How are you! I was just— Barry and I were just— discussing REPOsitories! Digital repositories! For code! Very professional! No naked witches involved!

*glances frantically at screen*

Can she see my face? Can she tell I was thinking about— about—

*mutters*

Stop thinking. Stop thinking. Stop thinking.
